title: centaurea achtarovii
intro: Centaurea achtarovii, also known as Achtarov's knapweed, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Balkan Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, providing relief from pain, swelling, and infections. The leaves and flowers of Centaurea achtarovii can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
